data.title: Target Stock (TGT) Rallies after Activist Investor Takes a Significant Stake
data.description:
Shares of Target (TGT) are rallying at the time of writing after the Financial Times reported that activist investor Toms Capital Investment Management had taken a significant stake in the retailer. As a result, investors appeared to welcome the possibility that outside pressure could lead to changes at the company. Although the exact size of the investment has not been disclosed, the news alone was enough to boost sentiment.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Unsurprisingly, this investment comes at a difficult time for Target, as it has seen 12 consecutive quarters of weak or flat sales growth. Meanwhile, the stock has fallen about 64% from its pandemic-era peak, when shoppers relied on Target as a convenient one-stop shop for essentials, clothing, and home goods. More recently, the slowdown has erased nearly a third of the company's share value this year, which has left Target well behind the broader retail sector.

At the same time, Toms Capital has been increasingly active across the market. Founded in 2017 by former GLG Partners executives, the firm has recently taken positions in companies such as Kellanova (K), U.S. Steel, and Kenvue (KVUE), where it has pushed for strategic changes. Therefore, investors will be watching closely to see whether the activist's involvement can help Target turn things around.

Is TGT Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TGT stock based on eight Buys, 12 Holds, and three Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TGT price target of $99.23 per share implies 1.2% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: U.S. Layoffs Surge by 54% to Highest Level Since 2020
data.description:
U.S. employers announced plans to lay off 71,321 employees in November, bringing the year-to-date count to 1.17 million, according to outplacement firm Challenger, Grey & Christmas. That's 54% more than the 11-month period a year ago and marks the highest amount since 2020 when the Covid-19 pandemic disrupted the global economy.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"It was the trend to announce layoff plans toward the end of the year, to align with most companies'
fiscal year-ends," said Challenger.

AI Advancements Could Spur Job Cuts

Last month, Verizon (VZ) announced its largest-ever round of layoffs with over 13,000 cuts. In October, Amazon (AMZN) said it would slash its corporate workforce by roughly 14,000 roles.

That comes as the unemployment rate rose to 4.6% in November, registering a four-year high. Looking ahead, analysts warn that layoffs may continue into the new year as AI developments accelerate the need for fewer workers.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: PLTR vs. BBAI: Wall Street Weighs Which AI Stock Has More Upside in 2026
data.description:
As AI stocks continue to grab attention, investors are also looking at companies tied to government, defense, and enterprise AI spending. In this space, Palantir Technologies (PLTR) and BigBear.ai (BBAI) have emerged as two closely watched names, each offering a different way to gain exposure to AI adoption. Using TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, we look at which stock analysts believe has more upside heading into 2026.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Is Palantir Stock a Buy or Sell Now?

Palantir has become one of the most visible AI stocks over the past year, supported by steady demand from government agencies and rising interest from commercial customers. Shares have surged 158% year-to-date, driven in part by growing adoption of the company's Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP). At the same time, long-term government contracts continue to provide a stable revenue base.

Analysts highlight improving margins, rising free cash flow, and expanding commercial revenue as key positives. Still, Wall Street remains cautious at current levels. Valuation is the main concern, with Palantir trading at a forward P/E of 268x, far above the sector average of about 25, suggesting much of the near-term growth may already be priced in.

That said, some analysts remain bullish on Palantir's longer-term outlook. For instance, top Bank of America analyst Mariana Perez Mora reiterated a Buy rating on Palantir and set a $255 price target, well above current levels. The analyst said Palantir's revenue growth shows no signs of slowing, fueled by strong demand from enterprise customers and expanding government contracts.

Is BigBear.ai a Good Stock to Buy Ahead of 2026?

BigBear.ai is a smaller and more speculative AI name, with a focus on defense, intelligence, and national security work. The company has benefited from rising interest in AI-driven defense tools as governments invest more in data analysis and decision-support systems. The stock has gained 49% year-to-date.

While revenue growth has improved, profitability remains a challenge. In the most recent quarter, BigBear.ai reported a net loss of $0.03 per share, narrower than the expected loss of $0.07. Adjusted EBITDA was negative $9.4 million, reflecting pressure from higher operating and SG&A costs.

From a valuation standpoint, BBAI trades at a price-to-sales multiple of about 13x, well above the sector median and its own historical average. However, it still trades below larger peers such as C3.ai (AI) and Palantir.

Wall Street analysts remain moderately bullish on BigBear.ai. They remain concerned about the execution risk due to the company's size and reliance on large government contracts, but believe that continued demand for defense-related AI work could support growth if the company executes well.

PLTR vs. BBAI: Which AI Stock Do Analysts Prefer?

Using TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, analysts currently show a more favorable view on BigBear.ai, which carries a Moderate Buy rating and an average price target of $6.50, implying about 8% upside from current levels. Meanwhile, Palantir is rated Hold, with an average price target of $187.87, suggesting around 3% downside.

While both stocks have delivered strong gains this year — BigBear.ai up nearly 49% and Palantir up about 158% — Wall Street appears more cautious at current prices, especially after the sharp rallies.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Gold, Silver Vault to Record Highs on Geopolitical Pressure, Rate Cut Hopes
data.description:
Both gold (XAUUSD) and silver (XAGUSD) rallied to new all-time highs on Friday amid rising geopolitical tensions and rate cut odds. Gold and silver are viewed as safe-haven assets and tend to perform well during times of geopolitical uncertainty.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On Thursday, the U.S. launched strikes against Islamic State members in Nigeria, while the Trump administration's blockade on sanctioned oil tankers leaving and entering Venezuela continues to be in effect.

"I have previously warned these Terrorists that if they did not stop the slaughtering of Christians, there would be hell to pay, and tonight, there was," Trump said in a Truth Social post.

How Do Lower Rates Affect Gold and Silver?

The Fed will announce its interest rate decision on January 28, 2026, and while the odds of a rate cut are low, they have been steadily growing in recent days and currently stand at 17.7% on CME's FedWatch tool.

"Prospect of lower U.S. interest rates is still supporting demand for gold and silver, lifting both metals to new record highs," said UBS analyst Giovanni Staunovo.

Lower rates benefit the two metals because they don't pay out interest. In other words, lower rates make gold and silver more attractive compared to interest-bearing assets, as the opportunity cost of holding the metals falls.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Nvidia's "Base Case" Is $250 by End of 2026, Says Dan Ives
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) remains at the center of the AI boom, and a top Wall Street analyst laid out his view for the stock into 2026. Wedbush Securities analyst Dan Ives said Nvidia's "base case" puts the stock around $250 by the end of 2026, as AI demand keeps rising and Nvidia holds a strong lead in AI chips and software.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ives' comments come as Nvidia is back in the spotlight, including reports that the company is moving closer to shipping its H200 AI chips to China, pending approvals. If cleared, the move could reopen a key market and support future revenue.

Ives Sees Nvidia at the Heart of the AI Buildout

Ives, a five-star analyst at Wedbush, has long been one of Nvidia's most visible bulls. He believes Nvidia's chips and software give it a clear edge as AI moves from early testing to real-world use. In the past, Ives has compared Nvidia's AI chips to "oil or gold," pointing to their key role in the tech space.

Despite a high valuation, Ives believes strong demand from cloud firms, data centers, and enterprises can support growth into 2026. He has also played down fears of an AI bubble, noting that many firms are still early in using AI at scale.

China Export Approval Is a Positive Catalyst

One key development that could support Ives' view is the U.S. government's recent approval of Nvidia's H200 AI chip exports to China. Ives has said access to China remains important for Nvidia's long-term growth, as it helps the company stay competitive in the global AI market.

He also noted that Nvidia's H200 chip remains ahead of many local options in terms of performance. While political risk has not gone away, Ives views any step toward broader market access as a positive for future demand.

Long-Term Growth Still in Focus

Looking ahead, Ives believes Nvidia will continue to benefit from steady spending on AI systems and wider use of its GPUs for both training and inference. He expects AI use to spread across more industries as companies move beyond early trials.

For investors, Ives' $250 base-case view for the end of 2026 reflects confidence that Nvidia can keep growing, even after the stock's strong run. In his view, Wall Street still sees room for gains if AI demand keeps building as expected.

Is NVDA a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Analysts remain highly optimistic about Nvidia's long-term outlook. On TipRanks, NVDA stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell rating. The average Nvidia price target of $263.58 implies nearly 40% upside potential from current levels. Year-to-date, NVDA stock has gained nearly 40%.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Trump and Zelenskyy to Hold Meeting with Peace Plan '90% Ready'
data.description:
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Friday that he expects to meet with President Trump over the weekend and discuss a 20-point Ukraine-Russia peace plan that "is about 90% ready."

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Zelenskyy added that Ukraine currently has no line of direct communication with Russia and that he favors the virtual presence of European leaders at the meeting, as the peace deal cannot be enacted without their approval.

Ukraine Pushes 20-Point Peace Plan as Talks Continue

Proposals under the 20-point plan include Ukraine receiving guaranteed sovereignty, security agreements, and economic recovery assistance. Zelenskyy noted that discussions surrounding control of Ukrainian territory and the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant are still in progress.

Last month, Trump proposed a 28-point plan to end the war between the two countries, although many considered the plan to be heavily skewed in Russia's favor.

data.title: U.S. Airlines Take a Plunge Despite Record Festive Traffic Forecasts
data.description:
Shares in leading U.S. airlines nosed lower today despite predictions that a record number of passengers will be taking to the skies over the festive period.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

United Airlines (UAL) was down 0.2% in pre-market trading, American Airlines (AAL) was 0.3% lower and Delta Air Lines (DAL) was off 0.16%.

More Passengers, Higher Prices

According to figures from the Airlines for America lobby group, a daily average of 2.9 million passengers will travel between December 19 and January 5. That is 1.5% more passengers than a year ago.

But it will come at a cost for passengers looking to get home or go on a break during the holidays.

Domestic airfares over the period will average $900 for a round trip, or 7% more than in 2024, the American Automobile Association said in a separate statement.

New York and New Jersey airports are expecting a record 5.7 million travelers from Dec. 22 to Jan. 4, a 1% increase from last year's numbers, according to the Port Authority of New York and New Jersey.

Chicago's airports are readying for nearly 4.8 million passengers expected to travel through O'Hare and Midway International Airport during the holiday season, a roughly 6% increase from last year's holiday season.

A Turbulent 2025

2025 has been a difficult year for the airline industry with a number of high-profile crashes, a government shutdown and economic uncertainty all impacting travel demand. However, share prices have held up relatively well – see below:

data.title: U.S. Defense Firms Slapped with Sanctions by China over Taiwan Arms Sale
data.description:
China has imposed new sanctions on 20 U.S. defense firms and 10 executives over their arms sales to Taiwan, including Boeing's (BA) St. Louis defense branch, Northrop Grumman (NOC), Red Cat Holdings (RCAT), and Anduril's Palmer Luckey.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"The Taiwan issue is the core of China's core interests and the first red line that cannot be crossed in China-U.S. relations," said a Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson on Friday.

Beijing Targets U.S. Defense Firms After Taiwan Weapons Sale

The defense firms will have their assets frozen in China and will be banned from transacting business with Chinese entities and individuals, while the executives will be blocked from entering China.

Last week, the Trump administration announced that it would provide Taiwan with $11.1 billion of weapons, including HIMARS rocket systems, drones, and Javelin anti-tank missiles. China's foreign ministry responded by saying that the deal "severely undermines peace and stability in the Taiwan Strait."
